mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in existing home sales and housing starts indicate a collapsing housing demand cycle, which transmits to broader equity valuations through financial sector stress.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS is below 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding corporate output capacity and rising profit margins, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month inflation exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing total nonfarm payrolls reflects strengthening economic activity and higher corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all macro regimes over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation spikes above 0.4% force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for 60 days following CPIAUCSL monthly jumps above 0.4% without subsequent rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and rising unemployment indicates a demand shock that erodes corporate earnings forecasts, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative returns when corporate profit margins expand by more than 2% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of industrial production contraction signals a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output and triggers equity sell-offs over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions fail to predict negative 20-day returns when global trade volumes expand.
